ane.title: The Ancient Near East
ane.description: The world of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: Mesopotamia, the Fertile Crescent, and Canaan.

egypt.title: Ancient Egypt
egypt.description: The great civilization along the Nile that enslaved and then released Israel, and later gave refuge to the infant Jesus.

exodus.title: Route of the Exodus
exodus.description: Israel's traditional route from Egypt through the Sinai wilderness to the plains of Moab.

canaan.title: Canaan Divided Among the Tribes
canaan.description: The allotment of the land among the twelve tribes of Israel after the conquest.

monarchy.title: The Kingdom of David and Solomon
monarchy.description: Israel at its greatest territorial extent under the united monarchy.
monarchy.commonsFile: File:MACCOUN(1899) p095 ABOUT 975 TO 930 B.C. - KINGDOM OF DAVID AND SOLOMON.jpg

empires.era: Divided Kingdom & Exile (1–2 Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah)
empires.title: The Assyrian & Babylonian Empires
empires.description: The great empires that conquered Israel (722 BC) and Judah (586 BC) and carried them into exile.
empires.commonsFile: File:Map of eastern Turkey in Asia, Syria and western Persia the period of the Babylonian and Assyrian empires (1915).jpg

neo_babylon.title: The Neo-Babylonian Empire under Nebuchadnezzar II
neo_babylon.description: The empire of Nebuchadnezzar II, who destroyed Jerusalem and its Temple and carried Judah into exile.

israel_ancient.era: Divided Kingdom & Exile (1–2 Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah)
israel_ancient.title: The Divided Kingdom
israel_ancient.description: The northern Kingdom of Israel and southern Kingdom of Judah as separate states, c. 830 BC.
israel_ancient.commonsFile: File:Kingdoms of Israel and Judah map 830.svg

persia.title: The Persian (Achaemenid) Empire
persia.description: The empire that ended the exile and under which Judah was restored as a province.

hellenistic.title: The Empire of Alexander the Great
hellenistic.description: The Hellenistic world that shaped the political and cultural backdrop of the intertestamental centuries.
hellenistic.commonsFile: File:Map of the Empire of Alexander the Great (1893).jpg

hellenistic_diadochi.title: The Successor Kingdoms (Diadochi)
hellenistic_diadochi.description: The rival Greek kingdoms — including Ptolemaic Egypt and the Seleucid Empire — that divided Alexander's empire and clashed over Judea for two centuries.

parthia.title: The Parthian Empire
parthia.description: Rome's great eastern rival, home to some of the "Parthians and Medes" present at Pentecost.

rome_trajan.title: The Roman Empire at Its Height (AD 117)
rome_trajan.description: The empire at its greatest territorial extent under Trajan, encompassing the entire New Testament world.

roman_judea.title: The Roman Province of Judaea
roman_judea.description: The Roman administrative world of the Gospels: Judea, Samaria, and Galilee under Rome.

christ_time.title: The Herodian Kingdom (Gospels Era)
christ_time.description: Galilee, Samaria, and Judea as the setting for Jesus's ministry.

paul1.title: Paul's First Missionary Journey
paul1.commonsFile: File:Paul the Apostle, first missionary journey.svg

paul2.title: Paul's Second Missionary Journey
paul2.description: From Asia Minor into Macedonia and Greece (Acts 15:36–18:22).
paul2.commonsFile: File:Paul the Apostle, second missionary journey.svg

paul3.title: Paul's Third Missionary Journey
paul3.description: Through Asia Minor and Greece again, ending toward Jerusalem and Rome (Acts 18:23–21:16).
paul3.commonsFile: File:Paul the Apostle, third missionary journey.svg

rome_divided.title: The Roman Empire Divided (AD 395)
rome_divided.description: The empire split into Western and Eastern halves a generation after Constantine legalized Christianity — the world the early Church entered as it grew across the empire.
rome_divided.commonsFile: File:Partition of the Roman Empire in 395 AD.png

israel_modern.title: Modern State of Israel
israel_modern.description: A contemporary relief map of the modern State of Israel.
